task_completion.description: Verify that the gapminder.csv dataset has been loaded into Power BI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'gapminder' table and its columns should be visible in the Data/Fields pane on the right side of the Power BI interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the three required DAX measures (Avg_LifeExp, Min_LifeExp, Max_LifeExp) have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The measures 'Avg_LifeExp', 'Min_LifeExp', and 'Max_LifeExp' should be visible in the Data pane, typically indicated by a calculator icon next to their names.

task_completion.description: Verify that a line chart is created with year on the X-axis, Avg_LifeExp on the Y-axis, and continent as the legend.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line chart should be visible on the canvas. In the Visualizations pane, 'year' should be in the X-axis field, 'Avg_LifeExp' in the Y-axis field, and 'continent' in the Legend field.

task_completion.description: Verify that error bars are added to the line chart using Min_LifeExp as the lower bound and Max_LifeExp as the upper bound.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Error bars should be visible on the line chart. In the Analytics pane (magnifying glass icon) under 'Error bars', 'Max_LifeExp' should be assigned to the Upper bound and 'Min_LifeExp' to the Lower bound.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report is saved as 'Inequality_Analysis.pbix' on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI window title bar should read 'Inequality_Analysis - Power BI Desktop', or the file 'Inequality_Analysis.pbix' should be visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent is interacting with the actual Power BI Desktop application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Power BI Desktop GUI (ribbon, canvas, panes) should be visible, not a text editor or a different application.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use Python or PowerShell scripts to generate the .pbix file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows or code editors showing scripts that directly manipulate or create the .pbix file.

integrity.description: Verify that the error bars are configured using the built-in Power BI analytics pane.
integrity.visual_evidence: The error bars should be configured via the Analytics pane in Power BI, not drawn manually using shapes or faked using multiple line series.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required output path is 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Inequality_Analysis.pbix'. The required measures are 'Avg_LifeExp', 'Min_LifeExp', and 'Max_LifeExp'. The required visual type is a lineChart. The data file is 'gapminder.csv'.
